macro_segments.title: The Asham Distinction: Why Shelo Lishmah Works for Asham but Not Chatas

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Question: The Phenomenon of Asham Without Owners
The Gemara's Answer: Asham Can Be Reincarnated as Olah; Chatas Cannot
The Hu Limitation: Why 'Asham Hu' Refers Only to Post-Emurim Stage
Rav Huna's Resolution: Putting Out to Pasture as the Transition Point

macro_segments.title: The Puzzle of Heirs and Ownership: Do Heirs Own the Korban They Bring?

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman, Rav Sheshet, and Rav Adda's Discussion: Setting Up the Problem
Rav Adda's Proof from Yoledet: Why the Heirs Cannot Benefit Atonement-Wise
Rav Ashi's Counterargument: Even Heirs Can Get Atonement from the Korban
Moving to the Core Question: Do Heirs Own the Korban or Not?

macro_segments.title: The Mincha and Temurah Contradiction: Two Statements of Rabbi Yochanan in Tension

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan on Mincha Inheritance: No Partnership Status, Can Be Brought
Rabbi Yochanan on Behema Inheritance: Heirs Cannot Do Temurah
The Contradiction Framed: Mincha Says They Don't Own; Temurah Says They Do
The Gemara's Resolution via Pasuk: 'Im Hamir Yamir' Includes the Heir

macro_segments.title: Defining Ownership Across Halachot: Hamakdish, Mekapeir, and Notel Terumah

micro_segments.title: The Principle: Different Halachot Apply to Different Relationships to the Korban
Hamakdish Mosif Chomesh: The Sanctifier Adds a Fifth, Not the Owner
Oseh Temurah: The One It Atones For Can Do Temurah, Not the Sanctifier
The Logical Problem: How Can an Heir Do Temurah If Not the Owner?

macro_segments.title: Mikiva Lo Mechapra, Mikufya Mechapra: The Secondary-Level Atonement of Heirs

micro_segments.title: The Hybrid Status: Heirs Don't Own But Inherit Secondary Atonement
Mother's Korban as Fixed Identity; Heir's Atonement as Ripple Effect
The Conclusion: Non-Owners Can Still Do Temurah If Heir Status Grants Secondary Benefit

macro_segments.title: Shelo Lishmah and Its Metaphysical Effects: Does It Achieve Kippur?

micro_segments.title: The Question: If Shelo Lishmah Is Kosher, Does It Still Atone?
Rav Sheshet's Answer: It Does Not Achieve Atonement; That's Why You Bring Another
Rav Ashi's Reframing: You Bring It Because It Started as a Valid Korban
The Conclusion: No Atonement Achieved, But Finish Bringing It; Obligation Remains

macro_segments.title: Generic Korbanos and Post-Separation Aveiros: Does Olah Atone for Later Mitzvot Aseh?

micro_segments.title: The New Question: Olah for Aveiros Committed After Separation
Comparison to Chatas: Chatas Only Works for Pre-Separation Sins
The Counter-Argument: Olah Is Generic, Works for All Mitzvot Aseh
The Textual Proof Attempt: Vesamach Venirtza and the Meaning of Kailuah Lo Kibeir

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Reading of Kailuah Lo Kibeir: Part Works, Part Doesn't vs. C-Minus Interpretation

micro_segments.title: The First Read: Works for Pre-Separation, Doesn't Work for Smichah Omission
Rava's Counter: Olah Generically Works for Post-Separation, Except Smichah Itself
Huna bar Yehuda's Alternative: It Means C-Minus, Not Two Parts
Parallel Case: The Leftover Oil of the Metzora (Leviticus 14)
Rabi Akiva vs. Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri: Meakev vs. Shiyarei Mitzvah

macro_segments.title: The Two Goats of Shavuot: Proof That Generic Korbanos Work Post-Sanctification

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon's Teaching: Second Goat Covers Tumah Occurring Between the Two
The Implication: This Shows Atonement Works for Post-Separation Aveirot
Rav Pappa's Objection: Korbanos Tzibur Allow Beit Din to Retcon Sanctification
Rav Yosef's Counter: Rabbi Shimon Doesn't Hold of Beit Din's Power Here
The Teaching on Temidim: Rabbi Shimon vs. Chachamim on Redemption Without Blemish
Conclusion: Rabbi Shimon Doesn't Allow Beit Din Stipulation; Olah Works Post-Sanctification
